articles.title: How the middle east crisis may affect Kerala election results 2026
articles.description: Kerala's 2026 assembly elections saw a record 78.27% voter turnout, with exit polls indicating a close contest between the UDF and LDF. Attention now turns to May 4th results, with questions arising about the potential impact of the Middle East crisis on the state's deep ties to the Gulf.

articles.title: BJP provoked unrest to force repolling in Bengal, alleges TMC's Panja

articles.title: Trump 'not satisfied' with new Iran proposal amid ceasefire stalemate
articles.description: US President Donald Trump said Friday he was "not satisfied" with a new Iranian negotiating proposal, as peace talks remain frozen despite a weeks-long ceasefire. "At this moment I'm not satisfied with what they're offering," Trump told reporters, blaming stalled talks on "tremendous discord" within Iran's leadership.

articles.title: West Bengal polls: Lockdown in strongroom areas after TMC, BJP clash
articles.description: Kolkata police have imposed strict prohibitory orders around EVM strongrooms following Trinamool's vote tampering claims and clashes with BJP workers. Security has been heightened with additional forces and sealed roads. The Election Commission vows to punish any disruption, emphasizing robust security at all counting centers statewide to ensure a peaceful electoral process.

articles.title: Quote of the day by Nicolas Cage: 'The end of the world is on people's minds. We have the power to destroy or...' – inspiring lessons on responsibility, human power and how our choices shape the world by Oscar-winning actor of Leaving Las Vegas
articles.description: Quote of the day by Nicolas Cage: Nicolas Cage emphasizes that humans possess immense power to shape the world, capable of both destruction and salvation. He highlights that our daily choices, not just grand actions, influence global outcomes. The actor's quote urges introspection on how we wield this power and responsibility, stressing conscious action over fear of the future.

articles.title: France Breaks With Washington, Demanding End to US Blockade Of Strait Of Hormuz
articles.description: The Western alliance is fracturing at the world’s most critical chokepoint. French Foreign Minister Jean-Noël Barrot has officially condemned the U.S. blockade of the Strait of Hormuz, calling it an illegal act that is punishing the global economy. As fuel prices skyrocket, France and the UK are moving to establish their own "neutral" naval mission—independent of President Trump’s command. With Washington telling Europe to "STAY AWAY," Paris is choosing strategic autonomy over a war it says it did not approve.

articles.title: Video: Row erupts over 'intoxicated' Bhagwant Mann. BJP, Congress attack Punjab CM, say 'same man who promises drug-free state'
articles.description: Punjab Chief Minister Bhagwant Mann is facing sharp criticism from leaders of the Bharatiya Janata Party and Indian National Congress over allegations that he appeared intoxicated in the Assembly. Opposition parties have described the incident as “shameful” and “disgraceful”, raising serious questions about his conduct and the governance of the state. The controversy has intensified even as Mann secured a confidence motion in the House.

articles.title: EC orders repolling in 15 polling booths in West Bengal on Saturday
articles.description: The Election Commission on Friday ordered repolling on May 2 in 15 polling stations in West Bengal, where voting was held on April 29 in the second phase of the assembly elections. In a letter to the Chief Electoral Officer of Bengal, the Election Commission (EC) said the repolling has been ordered based on inputs received from the state poll machinery and voting will be held from 7 am to 6 pm. While 11 polling stations are in the Magarhat Paschim assembly constituency, four are in Diamond Harbour. A report about complaints relating to the Falta constituency is awaited. A large number of complaints was received from the constituency. This is the first time that repolling has been ordered in the present set of assembly polls in West Bengal, Assam, Tamil Nadu, Kerala and Puducherry. No repolling was recommended in the first phase of the West Bengal polls held on April 23.
